name: Reconfigurable Antenna
description: An adaptive antenna system capable of dynamically altering its radiation characteristics including frequency, polarization, and beam direction through electronic or mechanical switching mechanisms. These antennas employ variable components such as PIN diodes, varactors, MEMS switches, or tunable materials to achieve real-time reconfiguration. They enable cognitive radio systems, adaptive beamforming, and multi-standard wireless communications.

switching_time.name: Switching Time
switching_time.description: Time required to reconfigure antenna characteristics

tuning_range.name: Frequency Tuning Range
tuning_range.description: Percentage frequency range over which antenna can be tuned

isolation.name: Port Isolation
isolation.description: Isolation between reconfigurable states or ports

insertion_loss.name: Insertion Loss
insertion_loss.description: Loss introduced by switching elements in active state

control_voltage.name: Control Voltage
control_voltage.description: Voltage required to activate switching elements

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves selecting reconfiguration mechanism, determining switching topology, optimizing antenna geometry for multiple states, and implementing control circuitry with minimal impact on radiation performance.

steps.title: Define Reconfiguration Requirements
steps.description: Specify frequency bands, polarization states, and beam directions needed

steps.title: Select Switching Technology
steps.description: Choose between PIN diodes, varactors, MEMS, or other switching elements

steps.title: Design Base Antenna Structure
steps.description: Create fundamental radiating element optimized for center frequency

steps.title: Implement Switching Network
steps.description: Integrate switching elements to modify current paths or reactive loading

steps.title: Optimize Multi-State Performance
steps.description: Tune antenna dimensions for acceptable performance across all states
steps.formula: VSWR_max = max(VSWR_state_i) for i=1 to N

steps.title: Design Bias Network
steps.description: Create DC bias circuitry with RF isolation using chokes and blocking capacitors

steps.title: Validate Switching Speed
steps.description: Verify reconfiguration time meets system requirements

equations.resonantFrequency: f_r = 1/(2*pi*sqrt(L_eff*C_eff)) where L_eff and C_eff vary with switch states
equations.inputImpedance: Z_in = Z_antenna + Z_switch_network = R_rad + j*X_reactive + Z_switching_elements
equations.gain: G = eta_rad * eta_mismatch * D where eta_mismatch = 4*R_rad*R_source/|Z_in + Z_source|^2
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta,phi) = sum(I_n * E_n(theta,phi)) where I_n depends on switch configuration
equations.bandwidth: BW = (f_high - f_low)/f_center where limits set by VSWR < 2 across all states

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Multi-state impedance matrix with switching element models: Z_total = Z_antenna + Z_switches(state)
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Superposition of current distributions for each switch state with method of moments integration
keyAssumptions: Switch elements modeled as ideal with parasitic reactances
Bias networks provide perfect RF isolation
Switching transients neglected in steady-state analysis
Mutual coupling between switches is minimal

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 21
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 20
Christodoulou, C.G. 'Reconfigurable Antennas for Wireless and Space Applications'
